hero.title: RICK AND
hero.subtitle: EXPLORING THE RICK AND MORTY MULTIVERSE

home.errorLoad: Could not load characters for this page.

empty.title: No characters found for these filters.
empty.hint: Adjust the name or filters and try again — the show’s official API returned no results for this combination.

about.skillsBody: I am a software developer with over nine years of experience building and evolving high-performance systems in fintech, SaaS, gaming, and health insurance. I work with JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, React, C# and .NET, microservices, and event-driven integrations (for example RabbitMQ), as well as clean code, SOLID, and CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins). I have strong experience with AWS cloud, Docker and Podman containers, security with OAuth2 and JWT, observability with Datadog, and game development with Unity and Godot. I value mentoring teams, designing scalable architectures, and agile collaboration in remote teams.

rpg.title: Character creation
rpg.subtitle: Spend 27 points (1:1 cost). Score before racial modifiers: 8–15. Total = sheet score + racial bonus + drawback.
rpg.characterNameHint: Shown on the sheet preview and included in the JSON export.
rpg.exportJsonAria: Download character sheet as JSON for an LLM session or backup
rpg.createCharacterAria: Open confirmation to finalize your character and view the full sheet
rpg.createBlockedHint: Spend all 27 points and enter a name before creating.
rpg.createBlockedAria: Create character (disabled until sheet is complete)
rpg.createConfirmDescription: You are about to lock in this build: race, name, point-buy, and all derived combat/social values. You can still export JSON afterward from the summary.
rpg.createdSubtitle: Here is your full sheet—stats, derived values, and racial skills.
rpg.createdStartGameHint: Coming soon — this button is a placeholder for now.
rpg.exportBlockedHint: Spend all 27 points before you can export.
rpg.exportBlockedAria: Export JSON (disabled until all points are spent)
rpg.exportBlockedNameHint: Enter a character name before you can export.
rpg.exportBlockedNameAria: Export JSON (disabled until a character name is entered)
rpg.exportConfirmDescription: A JSON file will download with your character name, race, abilities, and pool totals—ready to paste into an LLM session.
rpg.llmInstructions: Treat this JSON as the player's current Rick and Morty–style character sheet (meta.schemaVersion 3). Use character.name when addressing the player. Use abilities[].total and abilities[].d20Modifier (floor((total-10)/2)) for checks unless your table differs. Use derived for combat/social shorthand: hitPointsMax, physicalAttackRating (STR channel), magicalAttackRating (INT channel), socialInfluencePool (CHA, out of combat), extraStrikesBeforeEnemy / dexSpeedTier (DEX initiative cadence), stealthRating (DEX + stealthRacialBonus for subtle movement), stealthRacialBonus (0 or +2). Use race.skills (two attacks, support, item with outOfCombat) for per-race actions. For humans, human.playstyle describes generic item/build flexibility. rules.derivedFormulas explains the math strings. Respect race.drawbackModifiers and pointPool.

derivedFormulas.socialPool: socialInfluencePool = Charisma total (conversation and influence outside combat).
derivedFormulas.dexSpeed: extraStrikesBeforeEnemy from Dexterity total: 0 if DEX < 14, 1 for 14–15, 2 for 16–17, 3 if DEX ≥ 18 (extra player strikes before a typical enemy's first strike).
derivedFormulas.stealth: stealthRating = Dexterity total + stealthRacialBonus (0, or +2 for Bird-Person and Parasites—moving unseen, infiltration, tailing).

human.playstyleSummary: Humans may wield any item category your table allows and support many builds (martial, caster, face, skirmisher). Their racial +1s are flexible—re-flavor gear and stunts freely.

cheatSheet.title: Combat and social cheat-sheet
cheatSheet.description: How your final ability totals map to HP, damage channels, talk pool, stealth (moving unseen), and DEX attack cadence. Values update when you change scores or race.
cheatSheet.hitPointsHelp: Constitution total plus 10 — stamina and staying power.
cheatSheet.physicalHelp: Uses your Strength total as the physical damage rating.
cheatSheet.magicalHelp: Uses your Intelligence total as the magical damage rating.
cheatSheet.socialHelp: Uses your Charisma total for persuasion, bargains, and non-combat leverage.
cheatSheet.dexSpeedHelp: High Dexterity lets you resolve extra attacks before a typical enemy acts once. Tiers at DEX totals 14, 16, and 18.
cheatSheet.formulasToggle: Show formula text (for players and LLMs)
cheatSheet.stealthHelp: How hard you are to spot when sneaking, tailing someone, or bypassing guards—your Dexterity total is the base, with a small racial knack on some species.
cheatSheet.stealthStatusTitle: Where the stealth number comes from
cheatSheet.stealthDexLine: Base from your Dexterity total (after racial bonuses and drawbacks on the sheet): {{dex}}.
cheatSheet.stealthRacialLine: Racial knack toward staying unseen: +{{bonus}} (Bird-Person and Parasites get +2; everyone else +0).
cheatSheet.stealthRatingLine: Stealth rating used when the table resolves hiding or moving quietly: {{rating}} (= DEX total + racial knack).

ciancans.name: Ciancans
ciancans.imageAlt: Portrait of Prince Nebulon (Zigerion) from the show, used as visual reference for Ciancans.
ciancans.visualDescription: Scam-prone hive-minded aliens with green skin, red eyes, and fin-like frills—tech hustle energy.

attack1.name: Hive-Link Haymaker
attack1.summary: Coordinate a brutal strike through the hive-mesh; leans on Intelligence reads of the battlefield.

attack2.name: Simulation Spike
attack2.summary: Overlay a hostile AR burst that staggers foes who buy the fake telemetry.

support.name: Consolation Con
support.summary: Team-wide hustle: reroute blame, buy seconds, or spoof intent while allies reposition.

item.name: Pocket holo-suite
item.summary: Deploys a palm-sized scam projector for misdirection in a fight.
item.outOfCombat: Forge credentials, preview a room layout, or sell a 'limited-time' deal to NPCs without rolling initiative.

cronenbergs.name: Cronenbergs
cronenbergs.imageAlt: Portrait of Cronenberg Rick from the show, used as visual reference for Cronenbergs.
cronenbergs.visualDescription: Mutated bodies and exposed organic tissue in red, fleshy tones.

attack1.name: Bone-Spur Lunge
attack1.summary: A messy Strength-forward charge that leaves ichor splatter as cover.

attack2.name: Cronenberg Slam
attack2.summary: Ground-pound shockwave; good for crowding enemies into each other.

support.name: Horror Presence
support.summary: Intimidate and stall: foes hesitate, giving allies tempo to line up shots.

item.name: Mutagen maw
item.summary: Latch onto a foe to gnaw through armor or grapple tech limbs.
item.outOfCombat: Chew through rubble, sealed doors, or cable bundles—gross but fast demolition outside fights.

birdPeople.name: Bird-Person
birdPeople.imageAlt: Portrait of Birdperson from the show, used as visual reference for Bird-Person.
birdPeople.visualDescription: Avian humanoids with feathers, beaks, and a nimble silhouette.

attack1.name: Talon Flurry
attack1.summary: Two quick Dexterity strikes meant to bleed or strip guard.

attack2.name: Dive Spike
attack2.summary: Drop from above for piercing damage and knockback.

support.name: Thermal Draft
support.summary: Ride thermals to reposition an ally or clear hazard tiles mid-fight.

item.name: Wing-locked glaive
item.summary: A reach weapon tuned for spinning aerial passes.
item.outOfCombat: Fly recon: peek over walls, scout roads ahead, or reach ledges and catwalks no ladder can touch.

chuds.name: Chuds
chuds.imageAlt: Portrait of CHUD King from the show, used as visual reference for Chuds.
chuds.visualDescription: Subterranean equine humanoids—tough and tied to the underworld.

attack1.name: Tunnel Hoof Drive
attack1.summary: A Constitution-fueled stampede that ignores minor pain.

attack2.name: Royal Haymaker
attack2.summary: Crowd-control punch meant to stagger surface-dwellers.

support.name: Underking Rally
support.summary: Shout orders that grant temp grit to allies holding a chokepoint.

item.name: CHUD war pick
item.summary: Punch through stone plating or vehicle hulls in melee.
item.outOfCombat: Excavate shortcuts, pry blast doors, or carve climbing holds in tunnel stone.

cromulons.name: Cromulons
cromulons.imageAlt: Portrait of Armagheadon (Cromulon) from the show, used as visual reference for Cromulons.
cromulons.visualDescription: Giant planet heads with a strong, performative presence.

attack1.name: Headliner Beam
attack1.summary: Charisma-charged sonic blast—damage scales with how loud the crowd gets.

attack2.name: Judgment Drop
attack2.summary: Psychic weight that pins a target in place for an ally finisher.

support.name: Applause Shield
support.summary: Redirect praise into a brief barrier; great for protecting squishy teammates.

item.name: Orbital mic-stand
item.summary: Channel performance energy into melee arcs or ranged 'note' bolts.
item.outOfCombat: Host impromptu contests to sway crowds, stall guards, or earn safe passage with sheer spectacle.

parasites.name: Parasites
parasites.imageAlt: Portrait of Pencilvester (parasite) from the show, used as visual reference for Parasites.
parasites.visualDescription: Shapes that pose as friends until the real threat is revealed.

attack1.name: False Friend Shank
attack1.summary: Exploit trust for a sudden Dexterity stab from an assumed harmless form.

attack2.name: Memory Lash
attack2.summary: Intelligence attack that scrambles what the foe thinks they saw last round.

support.name: Parasite Chorus
support.summary: Spawn decoys that soak hits or confuse targeting algorithms.

item.name: Pocket parasite bud
item.summary: Detaches a mini mimic to harass flanks.
item.outOfCombat: Pose as mundane objects or 'helpful' NPCs to scout houses, offices, or ships without suspicion.

gearPeople.name: Gear People
gearPeople.imageAlt: Portrait of Anchor Gear (Gear-Person from Gear World) from the show, visual reference for Gear People.
gearPeople.visualDescription: Mechanical beings with exposed gears and bronze / metallic tones.

attack1.name: Geargrind Uppercut
attack1.summary: Spun gears add rending damage after a successful Intelligence read on weak points.

attack2.name: Clockwork Sweep
attack2.summary: Wide arc that jams enemy weapons or loosens armor bolts.

support.name: Field Strip
support.summary: Rapid maintenance: stabilize an ally bot, weapon, or vehicle subsystem.

item.name: Gear Wars toolkit
item.summary: Modular wrenches and torque calipers tuned for battlefield repairs.
item.outOfCombat: Decode ancient Gear World locks, tune hyperdrive couplings, or jury-rig traps from scrap.

gromflomites.name: Gromflomites
gromflomites.imageAlt: Portrait of Cornvelious Daniel (Gromflomite) from the show, distinct visual from the Ciancans race.
gromflomites.visualDescription: Insectoids with multiple eyes, usually in blue Galactic Federation uniforms.

attack1.name: Plasma Carbine Burst
attack1.summary: Disciplined three-round burst rewarded when you call shots with Intelligence.

attack2.name: Baton Riotline
attack2.summary: Close-range suppression meant to lock down hallways.

support.name: Formation Drone
support.summary: Deploy a tiny Fed drone that marks targets and feeds telemetry to the squad.

item.name: Fed restraint cuffs
item.summary: Energy manacles for pinning priority targets mid-fight.
item.outOfCombat: Process paperwork fast, bluff credentials, or cordon crime scenes while civilians watch.

humans.name: Humans
humans.imageAlt: Portrait of Morty Smith from the show, human visual reference for Earth (C-137) characters.
humans.visualDescription: Adaptable beings from Dimension C-137 (or not). Specialists at surviving interdimensional trauma.

attack1.name: Improvised beatdown
attack1.summary: Use whatever is nearby—chair, portal gun butt, laser screwdriver—as a flexible Strength strike.

attack2.name: Wildcard shot
attack2.summary: A desperate ranged attack that can be reflavored as tech or magic depending on your build.

support.name: Protagonist's Luck
support.summary: Once per long rest, force a reroll on a failed check for yourself or an ally you can see.

item.name: Omni-slot pack
item.summary: Humans may attune any mundane or exotic item your table allows without racial item locks.
item.outOfCombat: Swap loadouts freely: climb kits, translator buds, disguise cases—lean into whatever story beat you need.
